hook.text: A home router serves ten devices. An office Wi-Fi network serves thousands. The engineering is completely different.

buildup.text: Enterprise access points are centrally managed. A controller assigns channels, power levels, and security policies.

discovery.text: 802.1X authentication checks each user against a directory like Active Directory before granting access.

twist.text: Radio resource management prevents nearby access points from interfering with each other — auto-tuning in real time.

climax.text: Stadium Wi-Fi handles 60,000 fans streaming simultaneously. Hundreds of access points, carefully planned and tuned.

punchline.text: Enterprise Wi-Fi is an engineering discipline, not a consumer product.

quiz.question: What does 802.1X provide for enterprise Wi-Fi?
options: Individual user authentication against a directory service
Faster download speeds for all devices
